title: Mildly Fermented Russian Fish Poke
description: A pescatarian-friendly Russian dish featuring fermented fish, dashi, and a cheese-pull effect, served in a fondue pot for a solo dinner.

ingredients.name: fermentation spice mix
ingredients.notes: containing salt, sugar, and dill weed

instructions: Poach the cod fish in the dashi broth at 180 degrees Fahrenheit for 8 minutes, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 145 degrees Fahrenheit.
Remove the fish from the broth and let it cool to room temperature.
In a small bowl, mix the fermentation spice mix with the sour cream until well combined.
Place the cooled fish in a fondue pot and pour the sour cream mixture over it.
Sprinkle the grated cheddar cheese over the top of the fish and sour cream mixture.
Add the chopped scallions and a squeeze of lemon juice over the cheese.
Season with black pepper to taste.
Let the mixture ferment at room temperature for 2 hours, or until it reaches the desired level of fermentation.
Serve the mildly fermented Russian fish poke hot, with the cheese pull effect achieved by stirring the mixture gently.
